✓ Postgres 16 with pgvector extension
✓ Redis 7 with NOEVICT + TTL policy
✓ Ollama running with llama3:8b + gemma2:2b + embedding model
✓ Go service port 8080, Python gRPC port 50051
✓ All services health-checked and auto-restart
✓ Volume mounts for code reload
✓ All 3 devs can spin up fresh local env in 5 minutes

✓ agent.proto: AgentRequest message with all fields (session_id, message, options{})
✓ StreamAgent RPC (streaming response)
✓ InvokeAgent RPC (unary)
✓ All enums: EventType, AgentType, MemoryPolicy, SandboxBackend
✓ Generated Go code: agent.pb.go + agent_grpc.pb.go
✓ Proto validation: required fields, oneof constraints
✓ Unit tests: proto round-trip serialization

Title: Config loader + validation
Description: Load .env, validate all required fields at startup
Definition of Done:
✓ Support .env via godotenv
✓ Fail fast on startup if critical vars missing (POSTGRES_DSN, REDIS_ADDR, PYTHON_GRPC_ADDR)
✓ Type-safe config struct with defaults
✓ Env overrides for local dev (defaults point to docker-compose)
✓ Unit tests: valid config, missing required, type coercion
✓ Structured logging: startup config dump (no secrets)

Title: Postgres schema + migrations
Description: All tables: agents, agent_permissions, memory_summary, memory_entity, memory_workflow, tool_log, pending_approvals, graph_checkpoints
Definition of Done:
✓ agents table: agent_id, owner_user_id, system_prompt, tools[], sub_agents[], memory_policy, model, is_public, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at
✓ agent_permissions table: user_id, agent_id, role
✓ memory_summary table: user_id-scoped, pgvector index on embedding
✓ memory_entity table: user_id-scoped, unique constraint
✓ memory_workflow table: agent_id-scoped, cosine distance index
✓ tool_log table: indexed by tool_name+error for circuit breaker
✓ pending_approvals table: thread_id+tool, TTL via created_at
✓ Migration0001_init.sql with rollback capability
✓ Integration test: schema validates, migrations run idempotent

Title: Redis schema + TTL policies
Description: Define key patterns: session:{session_id}, session_lock:{session_id}, tool_error:{tool_name}
Definition of Done:
✓ session:{session_id} = []json with 7-day TTL
✓ session_lock:{session_id} = UUID with 30s TTL
✓ tool_error:{tool_name} = counter with 1-hour rolling window
✓ Configure Redis NOEVICT policy + max-memory-policy
✓ Helper functions: set_with_ttl, get_history, increment_error_counter
✓ Integration test: TTL expiration, lock release, counter resets
✓ Stress test: 1000 concurrent session writes, check no eviction

Title: Database initialization
Description: Create Postgres database, users, roles, and base setup
Definition of Done:
✓ CREATE DATABASE agent_harness
✓ CREATE ROLE dev_user, prod_user with appropriate permissions
✓ Set default schema search_path
✓ Configure connection limits per role
✓ Set statement_timeout, idle_in_transaction_session_timeout
✓ Enable necessary extensions: uuid-ossp (pre-pgvector)
✓ Unit test: database and roles created correctly
✓ Integration test: dev and prod users can connect

Title: pgvector extension setup
Description: Install and configure pgvector for vector embeddings
Definition of Done:
✓ CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector
✓ Verify pgvector version >= 0.5.0
✓ Test vector operations: <=> (cosine distance), @@ (inner product)
✓ Create vector type conversions for JSON embedding storage
✓ Benchmark: vector similarity query < 100ms on 100k rows
✓ Unit test: vector operations accurate
✓ Integration test: embeddings stored and queried correctly

Title: Connection pooling setup (pgbouncer)
Description: Configure pgbouncer for Go + Python connection pooling
Definition of Done:
✓ Install pgbouncer (local or K8s sidecar)
✓ Configure pgbouncer.ini: pool_mode=transaction, max_client_conn=1000, default_pool_size=20
✓ Set appropriate reserved_pool_size, reserve_pool_timeout
✓ Connection string: Go → pgbouncer:6432 → Postgres:5432
✓ Health check: validate pgbouncer connectivity from Go handler
✓ Metrics: active_connections, waiting_clients, server_login_time
✓ Benchmark: connection acquisition < 10ms p95
✓ Integration test: concurrent connections pooled efficiently

Title: Backup and restore procedures
Description: Document and test backup strategy with pg_dump and WAL archiving
Definition of Done:
✓ Full backup script: pg_dump with -F c (custom format)
✓ Incremental backups via WAL archiving (configure archive_command)
✓ Test restore procedure on separate DB instance
✓ Backup retention policy: daily for 7 days, weekly for 4 weeks
✓ Automate via cron job or K8s CronJob
✓ Monitor backup completion + size (alert if > 50% growth)
✓ Documentation: disaster recovery runbook with RTO/RPO targets
✓ Integration test: restore from backup completes successfully

Title: gRPC client pool
Description: Round-robin pool with keepalive and connection health management
Definition of Done:
✓ Pool size: GRPC_POOL_SIZE env var (default 10, configurable)
✓ Round-robin selection with atomic counter
✓ Keepalive: ping every 120s, 10s timeout, max 3 retries
✓ Graceful reconnect on broken connection
✓ Max recv message: 10 MB with validation
✓ Per-pool metrics: active_conns, failed_dials, reconnect_count
✓ Unit tests: round-robin distribution, keepalive timeout, reconnect
✓ Integration test: kill Python, verify Go reconnects within 30s
✓ Load test: 100 concurrent /chat requests, measure pool utilization
✓ Race condition test: 10 goroutines dialing simultaneously

Title: gRPC request builder
Description: Assemble AgentRequest from handler, registry, and Redis
Definition of Done:
✓ Fetch agent spec from cache by agent_id
✓ Check user has permission (agent_permissions table)
✓ GET session history from Redis, apply token counting
✓ If history > 75% of model context window: set compress_history=true
✓ Inject system_prompt, tools[], sub_agents[], tool_agent_map from spec
✓ Set memory_policy per-type enforcement
✓ Validate request: message non-empty, agent_id exists, user_id in JWT
✓ Build AgentRequest proto
✓ Unit tests: token boundary check, permission denied, missing agent
✓ Integration test: full request round-trip with real agent spec

Title: Agent registry cache
Description: Load from Postgres at startup, 30s refresh loop
Definition of Done:
✓ Query agents table at server startup
✓ Cache in sync.Map keyed by agent_id
✓ Background goroutine: every 30s, SELECT from agents WHERE updated_at > last_refresh
✓ Update cache with new/modified agents, TTL-delete old ones
✓ On-demand cache invalidation on POST/PUT/DELETE /agents
✓ Access control: validateAgentAccess(user_id, agent_id) before returning spec
✓ Metrics: cache_hits, cache_misses, refresh_duration_ms
✓ Unit tests: cache hit, miss, refresh, invalidation
✓ Integration test: create custom agent, verify cache picks it up within 30s
✓ Race test: simultaneous reads + refresh, no panics

Title: Session history in Redis
Description: Get/set history as JSON array, handle token bounding
Definition of Done:
✓ GET session:(session_id) from Redis
✓ Parse JSON array of prior turns (each: {role, content, timestamp})
✓ Return empty array on cache miss (not error)
✓ Handle malformed JSON gracefully
✓ Inject into gRPC AgentRequest.options.history[]
✓ Token counting: estimate tokens per turn
✓ If total > 75% of model.context_window, set compress_history flag
✓ Unit tests: cache hit, miss, malformed JSON, token boundary
✓ Integration test: 100 concurrent session reads, <5ms latency p95

Title: POST /chat SSE endpoint
Description: Main streaming chat handler with lifecycle hooks
Definition of Done:
✓ Parse request body: {message, model?, agent_id, conversation_context?}
✓ Authenticate JWT, extract user_id
✓ Validate: message non-empty, agent_id exists
✓ Generate request_id (UUID), start_time
✓ Trigger on_request_start hook: set compress_history if needed
✓ Call gRPC StreamAgent, stream responses as SSE to browser
✓ Set SSE headers: Content-Type, Cache-Control, Connection
✓ Buffer SSE events every 100ms or on event boundary
✓ Flush to ResponseWriter immediately on client disconnect
✓ Trigger lifecycle hooks: on_stream_event, on_tool_call, on_tool_result, on_stream_done
✓ Handle gRPC stream errors: log, close SSE stream gracefully
✓ Unit tests: request validation, SSE header format
✓ Integration test: 10-turn conversation with tool calls
✓ Load test: 50 concurrent /chat, p95 latency <800ms

Title: SSE frame serialization
Description: Convert gRPC events to JSON SSE format
Definition of Done:
✓ gRPC event types: text, thinking, tool_call, tool_result, approval_required, error, done
✓ SSE format: data: {json}\\n\\n with proper escaping
✓ Buffer events (time-window 100ms or frame-size 4KB)
✓ Flush on: boundary event (tool_call), timeout (100ms), size exceeds 4KB
✓ Append response_id header to each frame
✓ Test with quotes, newlines, emojis, binary data
✓ Unit tests: all event types serialized correctly, frame boundaries correct
✓ Integration test: browser receives frames in order, no corruption

Title: POST /agent/approve (HITL resume)
Description: Load checkpoint, resume with approval decision
Definition of Done:
✓ Validate user owns thread_id (from pending_approvals table)
✓ Load checkpoint from graph_checkpoints table
✓ Open new gRPC StreamAgent call with resume=true, thread_id, approved=true
✓ Stream response as SSE (reuse /chat SSE writer)
✓ Delete from pending_approvals on completion
✓ Handle checkpoint not found (404), expired (410), permission denied (403)
✓ Unit tests: authorization checks, checkpoint loading
✓ Integration test: approve tool call, verify graph resumes from exact node

Title: GET /agent/status endpoint
Description: Return approval/execution status by thread_id

Title: POST /agents endpoint (custom agent create)
Description: Validate and insert user-defined agent spec
Definition of Done:
✓ Parse request: agent spec JSON
✓ Validate all fields
✓ Check agent_id is unique per user (or globally if is_public)
✓ Insert into agents table with user_id owner
✓ Invalidate registry cache
✓ Return 201 + created spec + agent_id
✓ Unit tests: validation logic, unique check
✓ Integration test: CRUD cycle

Title: PUT /agents/:agent_id endpoint
Description: Update user-owned agent spec
Definition of Done:

✓ Apply same validation as POST
✓ UPDATE agents table row
✓ Invalidate registry cache immediately
✓ Return 200 + updated spec
✓ Unit tests: ownership, validation
✓ Integration test: verify cache invalidation

Title: DELETE /agents/:agent_id endpoint
Description: Soft delete user agent
Definition of Done:

✓ Invalidate registry cache

✓ List/GET endpoints filter out deleted=true

✓ Integration test: verify deleted agent not accessible

Title: GET /healthz endpoint

Definition of Done:

✓ Check gRPC pool has >= 1 healthy connection
✓ Check Postgres connectivity (query 1 arbitrary table)
✓ Both <1s timeout
✓ Return HTTP 200 if both OK, 503 if either fails
✓ Metrics: response time
✓ No database transaction needed
✓ Unit test: dependency checks

Title: Circuit breaker middleware
Description: Block malicious requests at HTTP layer (<1ms)
Definition of Done:
✓ Regex blocklist: SQL injection, common jailbreak patterns
✓ IP-based rate limiting: 50 req/s per IP, burst 100
✓ Embedding deny-list: detect + block vectorized attacks
✓ Trip → HTTP 400 + log, gRPC never called
✓ Metrics: blocked_count per rule
✓ Unit tests: patterns matched, false positives, latency <1ms
✓ Integration test: malicious payload blocked, benign request passes

Title: JWT Auth middleware
Description: Validate Bearer token, extract user_id
Definition of Done:
✓ Parse Authorization header
✓ Validate token signature (JWKS URL or static key per env var)
✓ Extract user_id claim
✓ Set in request context
✓ Return 401 if invalid/missing
✓ Unit tests: valid, invalid, expired tokens
✓ Integration test: middleware integration with router

Title: CORS middleware
Description: Origin allowlist with preflight request handling
Definition of Done:

✓ Handle OPTIONS preflight requests: 204 No Content
✓ Set Access-Control headers correctly
✓ Unit tests: allowed/denied origins
✓ Integration test: preflight + actual request

Title: Rate limiter
Description: Per-IP token bucket: 50 req/s, burst 100
Definition of Done:

✓ Return 429 when limit exceeded

✓ Metrics: rejected_count, current_rate_per_ip
✓ Unit tests: token bucket math, burst behavior
✓ Integration test: sustained load, burst accepted, then rejection

Title: Structured logger
Description: JSON logging with request_id, latency, user_id

✓ Fields: request_id (UUID), session_id, method, path, latency_ms, user_id (from JWT), status_code

Title: on_request_start hook
Description: Generate request_id, check history token count
Definition of Done:
✓ Generate UUID request_id
✓ Fetch session history from Redis
✓ Count tokens in history
✓ If > 75% of model.context_window: set compress_history=true
✓ Initialize in-memory tool_call_id map for this request
✓ Inject all into AgentRequest
✓ Unit tests: token counting, threshold check
✓ Integration test: compress_history propagates to Python

Title: on_stream_event hook
Description: Forward SSE frames to browser in real-time
Definition of Done:
✓ Receive gRPC stream events
✓ Convert to SSE format: data: {json}
✓ Append response_id header
✓ Write to http.ResponseWriter with flush
✓ No additional buffering at this stage
✓ Unit tests: SSE format compliance
✓ Integration test: frames arrive in order at <100ms latency

Title: on_tool_call hook
Description: Record tool invocation start time + args
Definition of Done:
✓ on_tool_call gRPC event: {id, name, args_json}
✓ Record in in-memory map keyed by tool_call_id
✓ Forward to browser as SSE
✓ Map keyed by tool_call_id for O(1) lookup
✓ Unit test: map insertion, lookup
✓ Integration test: on_tool_result finds the entry

Title: on_tool_result hook
Description: Compute duration, write tool_log audit entry
Definition of Done:
✓ on_tool_result gRPC event: {tool_call_id, content}
✓ Look up tool_call_id in in-memory map, get started_at
✓ Compute duration_ms = now() - started_at
✓ Async write to tool_log table
✓ If error content: set error=true flag, increment tool_error counter in Redis
✓ Forward to browser as SSE
✓ Cleanup: remove tool_call_id from map after write
✓ Unit tests: duration calc, error flag, counter increment
✓ Integration test: tool_log audit trail verified

Title: on_stream_done hook
Description: Fire 3 parallel memory writers + history write-back
Definition of Done:
✓ Receive done event: {eval_ok, summary, dag_json, entities[]}
✓ Spawn 3 goroutines: writeMemorySummary, writeMemoryWorkflow, writeMemoryEntities
✓ Each respects memory_policy: skip if 'never' or ('eval_ok_only' AND !eval_ok)
✓ Each implements retry logic: 3x with backoff, then log and continue
✓ If compress_history=true: replace Redis key with [done.summary] + 7-day TTL
✓ Write session history to Postgres
✓ Close SSE stream
✓ Total goroutine wait: allow up to 5s, then timeout
✓ Unit tests: goroutine spawning, no race conditions
✓ Integration test: verify all 3 writes complete, history write-back

Title: on_approval_required hook
Description: Store pending approval + notify user
Definition of Done:
✓ Receive approval_required event: {thread_id, tool, args}
✓ Insert into pending_approvals(user_id, thread_id, tool_name, args_json, created_at)
✓ Emit approval_required SSE frame to browser
✓ User ID from JWT token (not from event)
✓ Unit test: insert logic
✓ Integration test: user sees approval prompt

Title: writeMemorySummary
Description: Embed done.summary, upsert memory_summary
Definition of Done:
✓ Receive done.summary from gRPC event
✓ Call ollama embed endpoint (timeout 5s)
✓ Upsert into memory_summary(user_id, agent_id, embedding, text, created_at)
✓ Retry on Ollama timeout: 3x exponential backoff
✓ If all retries fail: log error, don't fail user request
✓ Postgres connection: spawn + close per write
✓ Unit tests: embedding call, upsert logic
✓ Integration test: real Postgres + Ollama

Title: writeMemoryWorkflow
Description: Embed + deduplicate, insert memory_workflow
Definition of Done:
✓ Receive done.dag_json from gRPC event
✓ Embed first 200 chars as query proxy
✓ Deduplication: skip insert if cosine distance < 0.1 to existing
✓ If new: INSERT into memory_workflow(agent_id, query_embedding, dag_json, created_at)
✓ Retry on failure: 3x backoff
✓ Unit tests: cosine distance calculation, dedup logic
✓ Integration test: verify dedup threshold behavior

Title: writeMemoryEntities
Description: Embed entities, batch upsert memory_entity
Definition of Done:
✓ Receive done.entities[] from gRPC event
✓ Batch: collect all entities
✓ For each: embed name + type
✓ Batch HTTP call to Ollama (one request with all vectors)
✓ For each: UPSERT memory_entity(user_id, entity_name, entity_type, embedding, context_text, last_seen)
✓ On conflict: update last_seen + context_text
✓ Retry batch on failure: 3x backoff
✓ Unit test: upsert + conflict logic
✓ Integration test: batch embedding + upsert

Title: Session history write-back (Postgres)
Description: Replace Redis history with Postgres copy
Definition of Done:
✓ If compress_history=true: insert into old_history_archive table
✓ Postgres INSERT: session_id, history_json, created_at, compressed_at
✓ No vector needed, plain JSONB
✓ Clean up old archive rows > 30 days via background job
✓ Unit test: insertion logic
✓ Integration test: history appears in Postgres after stream close

Title: History compression check (token counting)
Description: Estimate tokens, set compress_history flag
Definition of Done:
✓ Estimate tokens: sum(len(message.content).split().len * 1.3) per turn
✓ Check against model.context_window from map
✓ If total > 75%: set compress_history=true in AgentRequest
✓ Hardcoded model context windows: gemma2:2b (8192), llama3:8b (8192), mistral:7b (32768)
✓ Fallback: 4096 for unknown models (conservative)
✓ Unit test: token estimate accuracy vs. actual
✓ Integration test: compress_history propagates correctly
